source.name: Chrome DevTools Remote Debugging
source.license_notes: Summarized guidance with source link; do not copy full docs.

goal: Prevent remote debugging from becoming a local security risk.
steps: Use a dedicated automation profile when possible.
Avoid leaving remote debugging browser open after task.
Do not share WebSocket endpoint or expose port through firewall/tunnel.
Pause before destructive actions, purchases, deletes, or publishing.
safety_notes: Treat CDP as high privilege browser control.
Do not automate payments/deletion/publishing without explicit confirmation.

quality.notes: Security guidance synthesized from CDP capability and local automation risk.

retrieval.ai_summary: CDP is powerful browser control. Bind remote debugging to 127.0.0.1, use dedicated profiles where possible, never expose the WebSocket endpoint, and stop before irreversible actions.
